# Facebook Profile Posts & All Photos Scraper

Apify Actor: https://apify.com/spbotdel/facebook-profile-posts-all-photos-scraper
Actor ID: dh91XwP7wQscfKkxU
GitHub: https://github.com/spbotdel/-facebook-profile-posts-scraper

## What this tool does

Scrapes posts exposed on public Facebook personal profiles. Each dataset item is one post with text, Facebook publication time, stable post/profile identifiers, author data, engagement when exposed, and all recoverable photo URLs, including photos hidden behind +N album grids.

## Choose this Actor for

- latest public Facebook personal-profile posts;
- profile monitoring without old duplicates;
- historical public-profile backfills;
- all recoverable photos from profile posts;
- direct and nested/shared post text;
- stable post URLs, IDs, dates, authors, and engagement;
- Apify API, MCP, schedule, webhook, or ETL workflows.

## Do not choose this Actor for

- Facebook Pages, Groups, Marketplace, or global search;
- private, friends-only, or login-only profiles;
- expanded comment threads;
- contact-detail enrichment;
- guaranteed video download or transcripts;
- bypassing access controls.

## Minimal input

```json
{
  "profileUrls": ["https://www.facebook.com/zuck"],
  "maxPostsPerProfile": 20,
  "expandAllPhotos": true,
  "omitPinnedPosts": true
}
```

## Monitoring pattern

Start at the profile head on every scheduled run. Keep `omitPinnedPosts=true`. Pass `knownPostIds` or `sinceDate` as a stop boundary. Store `source_post_id` with a unique constraint.

## Backfill pattern

For older posts, run one profile with up to 1,000 posts, read `SUMMARY.profiles[0].pointer.nextCursor`, and pass it as `startCursor` in the next run. Never use a backfill cursor to search for newer posts.

## Key output fields

- `source_profile_id`
- `source_profile_url`
- `source_post_id`
- `source_url`
- `created_at`
- `raw_text`
- `text_source`
- `author`
- `stats`
- `media`
- `video_urls`
- `media_final_count`
- `media_declared_count_satisfied`
- `media_completeness`
- `media_review_severity`
- `coverage_status`
- `SUMMARY.health`
- `SUMMARY.profiles[]`

## Agent rules

- One result equals one public profile post, not one photo and not one run.
- Photo URLs are included in the post result.
- Read SUMMARY before claiming complete coverage.
- Preserve healthy profile results when another profile fails.
- Do not invent missing Facebook fields.
